meaning: n.漩涡,涡状形vt.使成漩涡vi.打漩,盘绕,头晕vi.<口>挥击,大口喝酒
examples: Long vines swirl round the tree.
The reason for this swirl of maritime outlawry can be found on the nearest shore.
If he/she goes into a negative swirl, ignore or give a simple "I see' or 'OK" reply.
The bus rounds a corner and chugs to a stop in a swirl of silicate dust.
Concerns about showrooming (or pesky crowds and limited parking) can be (somewhat) dismissed according to Swirl.
